ideas.title: Multigrid-accelerated self-consistent phonon charge dynamics for large-scale periodic solids
ideas.description: This research fuses multigrid solvers from computational fluid dynamics with an automated perturbative framework for phonons in crystals. The core innovation is to apply the multigrid hierarchy—traversing from fine to coarse grids—to solve the Poisson equation for the electronic charge density response to ionic displacements across the Brillouin zone. This directly attacks the major bottleneck in extending such perturbative methods to solids with many atoms per cell or complex phonon wavevectors, where traditional iterative solvers converge prohibitively slowly due to long-range Coulomb interactions. The resulting method will enable high-throughput, first-principles studies of anharmonic effects, phonon-mediated superconductivity, and lattice instabilities in materials like moiré superlattices, high-entropy alloys, and complex oxides, which are currently beyond reach.

ideas.title: Adapt Fast Multipole Methods from Astrophysics for Boundary Element Charge Integration
ideas.description: This idea proposes adapting the Fast Multipole Method (FMM), a cornerstone technique from astrophysics for efficiently computing long-range gravitational forces in N-body simulations, to the boundary element method (BEM) context for surface charge integration in electromagnetics. The analogous astrophysical problem is the O(N²) scaling of direct force calculations, solved by FMM using hierarchical spatial decomposition (e.g., an octree) and multipole/local expansions to approximate interactions between distant clusters. The adapted research idea applies this hierarchical approximation framework to BEM. By clustering boundary elements and representing the influence of distant clusters via their multipole moments (rather than computing all element-to-element interactions directly), the computational complexity of constructing and solving the BEM system matrix is drastically reduced from O(N²) to near O(N). This would enable highly accurate surface charge modeling on vastly more complex and detailed geometries than currently feasible with standard BEM solvers.

ideas.title: Adapt CP- and Tucker-based tensor completion for sparse force constant data
ideas.description: The original idea proposed applying tensor decomposition to compress high-order force constant data. By drawing an analogy to recommender systems, where CP and Tucker decompositions are used for tensor completion on sparse user-item-rating data, we can adapt this solution. The adapted research idea is to use these tensor factorization techniques not just for compression, but for active management and prediction of force constants. Specifically, by modeling the known force constants as sparse tensor entries, a low-rank model can be learned and used to complete the tensor, predicting missing anharmonic couplings. This approach would allow for the efficient and accurate construction of complete force fields from a limited set of expensive quantum mechanical calculations, directly parallel to how recommender systems predict missing ratings from sparse user interactions.

ideas.title: Apply Multiresolution Analysis from Image Processing to Solvation Charge Convergence
ideas.description: This idea adapts multiresolution analysis techniques from image processing to accelerate iterative solvation charge calculations. In image processing, problems like denoising are solved efficiently by first working on a coarse, low-resolution version of the image to capture broad features, then refining the solution at finer scales. Adapted to the research context, the solvation charge density field is decomposed into multiple resolution levels. The iterative solver first converges rapidly on a coarse grid to approximate the long-range electrostatic response. This coarse solution is then interpolated and used as a highly accurate initial guess for iterations on finer grids, systematically reducing the number of required iterations at the most computationally expensive, finest resolution. This approach mirrors the efficiency gains of multigrid methods but is inspired by the scale-space processing paradigms of computer vision.

ideas.title: Physics-Constrained Neural Solver for Self-Consistent Polarization Charges
ideas.description: This approach embeds a neural network predictor within a framework that enforces strict physical constraints. A small, guaranteed-consistent corrective solver projects the network's initial charge guess onto the manifold of physically admissible solutions. The entire pipeline—network and constraint projector—is trained end-to-end using a composite loss function that includes both data fidelity and the residual of the governing electrostatic equations. This ensures robust, physically meaningful predictions even for novel configurations, maintaining stability and accuracy where a pure black-box network would fail.
